But at the same time, for police detective Si-yeon, it is a place she is unjustly demoted to for doing her job. The two team up to arrest the rich and powerful speed maniac Jae-chul who is suspected of committing crimes for his obsession for speed.

programme.title: The Lightkeepers
programme.desc: Seth Atkins (Academy Award Winner, RICHARD DREYFUSS) is the light keeper on a deserted outer beach of Cape Cod in the year 1912. Seths assistant suddenly quits, no longer able to tolerate his bad attitude and hate for women. He is left to manage the lighthouse on his own, until a mysterious, strapping young man washes up on shore with the desire to become Seths new assistant. They eventually swap tales from their past, beginning to explain each of their distaste for women. Its not long before the two men must contend with the lovely Ruth Graham (MAMIE GUMMER) and her housekeeper, Mrs. Bascom (Golden Globe Nominee BLYTHE DANNER) who move into a nearby cottage for the summer.

programme.title: Bloomberg Technology
programme.desc: Technology is not only becoming an increasingly important part of people's lives but also of the American economy. "Bloomberg Technology" takes a look at how the latest happenings in the tech industry are affecting the U.S. markets. Analysis, commentary, interviews and interactive graphics are among the staples of the show that help viewers understand the impact the technology sector has on the economy. The show, which was originally titled "Bloomberg West," is anchored by Emily Chang in San Francisco.

programme.title: Bloomberg Real Yield
programme.desc: Not everyone has a lot of money to invest, which is why Bloomberg has financial indices that are based on fixed income, which is also the focus on this weekly show. The program covers news and trends in the fixed-income market and includes interviews with guests from major financial institutions. "Bloomberg Real Yield" incorporates data and analysis from terminal bond experts to help dive into topics that are moving the bond markets in order to help empower viewers to make knowledgeable financial decisions.

programme.title: Maata Ki Mahima
programme.desc: In the divine saga inspired by Bhakt Prahlad, witness the journey of Mahima, a devout disciple of Maata, as she challenges the tyranny of Indrayani, whose sins overflow in a reign of oppression. Sent into the heart of Indrayani's lineage, Mahima embodies the triumph of faith over darkness, destined to end the era of tyranny and usher in an era of righteousness.

programme.title: Stories By Rabindranath Tagore
programme.desc: The Characters Of Tagore'S Stories Spring Into Life Through The Cinematic Imagination Of Acclaimed Filmmaker, Anurag Basu In ‘Stories By Rabindranath Tagore'.Tagore'S Words And Basu'S Vision Have Amalgamated Into A Show That Transports You Back In Time To A World As Complicated As This One But With Far More Appeal And Beauty.

programme.title: Dahej Daasi
programme.desc: This Story Is About A 19Year Old Girl Named Chunari, Who Dreams Of Marriage And A Happy Family. However, She Becomes Entangled In The Chains Of Deceit, Finding Herself On The Path To Becoming A Dahej Daasi, Which Is A Tradition Still Prevalent In The Backward Regions Of Rajasthan. The Show Unfolds Into A Compelling Drama Of Tradition Versus Rebellion, Love Versus Oppression.

programme.title: Dhartiputra Nandini
programme.desc: Dhartiputra Nandini Tells The Story Of A Farmer Girl Who Wants To Stay Connected To Her Land. At The Same Time, Akash Returns From America. Sumitra Devi Wishes That His Grandson Akash Should Take Over The Entire Business By Becoming “Ceo.” At The Same Time, Her Family Is Falling Apart, And She Needs An Ideal Daughter-In-Law Who Understands The Responsibility Of Being Her Daughter-In-Law. She Sees Nandini As That, But Akash Refuses To Accept Her Since She Is Not Educated. Nandini Proves That She Is The Best, Both As A Supporter And A Life Partner. She Is There For Akash'S Family In Every Circumstance. This Is The Journey Of “Dhartiputra Nandini” From “Life Partner For Life” To “Charioteer.” In Addition, She Manages His Business. Nandini Also Fulfills Akash'S Dreams And Makes The Name Of The Country Known To The Entire World

programme.title: Laal Banarasi
programme.desc: The Story Highlights The Exploitation Happening With The ‘Bunkars' In Banaras, Whose Craft Is Appreciated All Around The World, But They Are Paid Peanuts By The Traders. Amidst This, Blooms A Love Story Between The Two Opposites – Gauri, A Bunkar And Garv, A Trader. The Story Is Of Gauri Who Belongs To The Bunkar Community Facing A Series Of Incidents That Destroy Her Family And Swears To Bring About A Change In Their Life And Stop The Exploitation By Directly Trading The Sarees. This Becomes A Battle With Traders Creating Hurdles In Her Life But She Is Unstoppable.

programme.title: Janani
programme.desc: When Children Are Young, Parents Care For Them And Give Them All They Need To Live A Good Life. The Circle Of Life, However, Means That One Day, It Is The Children'S Turn To Care For Their Parents And Give Them A Happy Retired Life. In The Current Moral Fabric Of Society, Rarely And Not Often It Works Out That Way. It Didn'T Happen That Way With Savita Too, As Her Children Had No Time To Look After Her. So She Decided Not To Settle Out And Reinvent Her Life. She Embarks On A Journey To Discover Herself And To Make Her Own Identity As An Individual. Janani Tells An Inspirational Story Of A Mother Who Rose Against All Odds And Became Successful.

programme.title: Crime Files
programme.desc: It All Begins Around Us This Crime Show Explores Crimes That Occur Within Our Own Communities And Among People We Know. It Tells The Compelling Stories Of Ordinary Lives Disrupted By Crime, With Relatable Characters Representing The World Around Us. The Show'S Mission Is To Raise Awareness About Crime Prevention While Keeping Viewers Engaged And Intrigued."

programme.desc: is a lively and comedic story of Jerry's birthday celebration. Jane surprises Jerry with a cake, but playfully keeps it out of his reach, leading to a chase around the house. Amidst the chaos, the cake ends up on the fan, creating further obstacles. Jerry and Jane search desperately for the cake, leading to a comical scene of mistaken discoveries. As the clock strikes midnight, signaling the end of Jerry's birthday, they become disheartened. However, a spontaneous cake mishap involving the fan brings laughter and joy to both characters, turning the situation into a hilarious and memorable moment

programme.title: C4TNA_OneNightOnly_Xtravaganza_EN
programme.desc: Impact Wrestling is a professional wrestling television program for Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA). As of 2005, it airs weekly on Spike in the United States and Canada. Since it's inception Impact Wrestling has primarily been filmed at the Impact Wrestling Zone in Orlando, Florida.

programme.title: F6_DCS_19___Chase_vs_Roberts
programme.desc: Dynasty Combat Sports is Nebraska's largest and most active Mixed Martial Arts Company and will hold its 94th event over the past 7 years. UFC fighters such as Darrick Minner, Jason Brilz, Ryan Jensen and Grant Dawson had all competed in the DC Cage.

programme.title: B7THH_ep02
programme.desc: Launched in 2010 as a brand new boxing platform for British based boxers to compete on, the Queensbury Boxing League has firmly established itself as one of the UK's most exciting boxing competitions. Divided into nine separate weight categories and five skill levels, boxers battle it out over three, four and five two minute rounds against opponents of equal skill and ability in their respective weight classes and move up and down the rankings depending on their results. The most successful competitors will earn the right to challenge for one of the five separate titles in each weight division starting with the novice title and moving all the way up to the coveted Queensbury British title for the most skillful. The action is fast, exciting and competitive, with the UK's premier boxing league continuing to give boxing fans the most entertaining nights of action in the country.

programme.title: Dropped
programme.desc: With base camp set up and the cold setting in, Chris and Casey must find food…fast. Their energy levels are depleting as they begin their hunt for moose. As time ticks on, the brothers must think on their feet to find food…or risk going hungry.

programme.title: Dropped
programme.desc: Saskatchewan's bitter cold and unforgiving wind makes for brutal days and nights for Chris and Casey. A rainstorm adds more difficulty and discomfort. Mentally drained and hungry, the brothers must try new tactics to find food and warmth.

programme.title: Dropped
programme.desc: The lack of hunting opportunities have drained Chris and Casey's patience. Arguments ensue when they disagree over staying put, or portaging to a new lake. Signs of a bear remind the brothers that they must remain focused in order to stay alive.

programme.title: Dropped
programme.desc: Symptoms of starvation have set in for Chris and Casey, who haven't eaten in days. When they cross paths with a grouse, the unexpected happens. Frustration peaks as the temperatures drop. The brothers set up a new camp, but will it help the hunt?

programme.title: Dropped
programme.desc: With still no big game in sight, Chris and Casey are forced to rely on fishing for food. The cold temperatures have slowed down the fish, creating challenges. When Mother Nature brings a brutal snow storm, the brothers are tested like never before.

programme.title: Dropped
programme.desc: After weeks of trying to survive in Saskatchewan, Chris and Casey have yet to kill big game. Time is running out as the bear begin to hibernate. Mental willpower is wearing thin. But a message from back home changes everything.

programme.title: Dropped
programme.desc: A turn of events lifts the brothers' spirits, but creates a huge hurdle they must overcome as they enter a new race against the clock. Chris and Casey reflect on the challenges they have faced so far, and the challenges that still lie ahead.

programme.title: Dropped
programme.desc: In the exciting season finale, Chris lays it all on the line one last time while bear hunting. Casey must finish one final task to make the fruits of their labor worthwhile. As the brothers inch closer to safety, they wonder what the future holds.

programme.desc: Rick Harrison and his family own and run a pawn shop in Las Vegas. They purchase, sell and evaluate interesting artefacts with fascinating stories.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Kenny ponders over his past memories while Jarrod and Brandi have to fix a tool locker and Darrell with the help of his granddaughter comes across an unusual find.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Professional buyers go to auctions and explore storage units in search of lucrative and interesting items that could fetch them a big profit.

programme.title: Forged In Fire
programme.desc: Top bladesmiths from different walks of life compete to create the best weapons and prove their effectiveness in a series of daunting challenges.

programme.title: History's Greatest Escapes with Morgan Freeman
programme.desc: Celebrated actor and host Morgan Freeman revisits some of the most daring jailbreaks conducted by ingenuous prisoners from across the globe.

programme.title: How Sex Changed The World
programme.desc: The film-makers focus on the fearless men and women who risked everything to reveal the mysteries behind sex. These include figures such as Leonardo da Vinci and the man who translated the Kama Sutra.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A comprehensive compilation of some of the best episodes from the reality show 'Pawn Stars', in which the Harrison family buys and sells fascinating artefacts in their family-owned pawn shop.

programme.title: Food Tech
programme.desc: Host Bobby Bognar visits Las Vegas to explore various buffet dishes, catch shrimp and make cocktail sauce. Later, he also gains insight into the making process of honey.

programme.title: Tyohaar Ki Thaali
programme.desc: Sakshi Tanwar in Tyohaar Ki Thaali decides to celebrate one festival a week and along with the celebration, she also prepares two dishes from her mom's diary which is her prized possession. Along with preparing the dishes she also reminisces into the moments of how she celebrated some festivals and also shares some stories about the Gods.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded
programme.desc: This series will go back through the record to explore life-altering, earth-shattering, unforgettable events using a combination of carefully selected archive, cutting edge CGI and informative scripting in this popular science format. As well as exploring spectacular archive, illustrating the full toll these disasters can take, we'll dig into the science behind each event, shining a light on the most fascinating elements and giving an understanding on how and why these events occur

programme.title: Ekaant
programme.desc: India is known for its remarkable architecture and serene landscapes. Its rich history is steeped in mystery its vastness - lends itself to several stories and urban legends. Ekaant takes you through the abandoned roads and alleys of some of India's historic locations. Travelling through the spread out palaces and deserted forts, the show documents and investigates the eerie processes of abandonment of each site. How flourishing towns and cities came to be in ruins, is the question each episode seeks to answer. Ekaant explores the ceasing of life and the triumph of nature even in the most populated of places and takes you on the road no longer travelled.

programme.title: Mid
programme.desc: Veteran actor, Naseeruddin Shah, explores the many untold stories of Indian cricket. He recalls the history of the game match highlights. Sportsmanship, stadiums, hits and misses, and many more. In his unique narrative style, Naseeruddin Shah brings alive the humorous side of the game, along with undocumented anecdotes and quirky trivia. The show also features many personalities who narrate cricketing experiences and stories including Shashi Tharoor, Narl Contractor, Bishan Singh Bedi and Charu Sharma amongst others

programme.title: The Way Back Home
programme.desc: Rohan Thakur finds his true calling as he begins a journey to revisit his roots in Himachal Pradesh. Way Back Home is as much a travelogue of childhood, as of growing up. Get ready for a journey of a lifetime

programme.title: Regiment Diaries
programme.desc: The Indian Army is an emotion – a living chronicle of events, that we have the luxury to forget because they remember. The army itself is a family of several regiments, each of which has its own history, identity, and glorious traditions. Regiment Diaries is a tale of the Indian Army unlike any told before. Told by the very men who follow the illustrious feats of their predecessors, it tells timeless tales that warm the heart, moisten the eye, and leave you in awe of the bravest children of India.

programme.title: Tyohaar Ki Thaali

programme.title: Trajectory
programme.desc: BEYOND EARTH ORBIT - The Apollo program takes bold new steps. IXV RE-ENTRY VEHICLE - Europe is working to perfect a re-entry vehicle. GALILEO SATELLITES LOST AND FOUND - Navigation satellites go into incorrect orbit.

programme.title: Trajectory
programme.desc: TO THE MOON - The Apollo program achieves its major objective. DRAGON, FALCON AND SPACEX - New directions in re-usable spacecraft. GAIA - Mapping our galaxy in unprecedented detail.

programme.title: Trajectory
programme.desc: THE BEGINNING OF THE END - After the first Moon landing the Apollo science program was about to start in earnest but people were losing interest. APOLLO GETS COMPLICATED - New technology is added that extends the reach of the Apollo program.

programme.title: Trajectory
programme.desc: THE SHUTTLE PROGRAM - The first re-useable spacecraft. THE CHALLENGER DISASTER - Pressing launch schedules lead to carelessness and the loss of the Challenger. RETURN TO SPACE - A re-designed shuttle is back in space with a redefined role.

programme.title: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Asteroids, the debris from the early solar system hold tantalizing clues to planetary formation. The spacecraft Psyche readies to study a unique metallic asteroid. As Lucy is on its way to the trojan asteroids that share Jupiter's orbit.

programme.title: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Juice and the Europa Clipper are two missions to our Solar System's largest planet. They will spend over four years studying Jupiter's atmosphere, magnetosphere and in particular its icy moons, investigating if they hold oceans suitable for life.

programme.title: The New Frontier
programme.desc: The James Webb Space Telescope is now operational. Launched aboard an Arianne 5 the 10-billion-dollar spacecraft has deployed successfully and reached its orbit around the Sun. Soon Webb will make some findings and pose new questions to be answered.

programme.title: The New Frontier
programme.desc: With the new satellite IXPE high energy telescope we will be looking at some of the most dramatic, and most violent objects in the universe: Colliding Black holes and Neutron Stars. The x-ray vision will reveal their hidden secrets.

programme.title: The Lightbulb Moment
programme.desc: In 1841 on the small island of Réunion, a 12-year-old slave did something no one had done before artificially pollinate the vanilla plant. The vanilla bean took the world by storm, sparking the artificial flavor revolution and changing how we eat.

programme.title: The Lightbulb Moment
programme.desc: Actress Hedy Lamarr, “the world's most beautiful woman,” had a knack for inventing. During WWII, in an effort to help the Allies, she devised a technology designed to intercept Axis communications – and created the foundation of wireless internet.

programme.title: The Lightbulb Moment
programme.desc: The early days of online video were plagued by low resolution and unbearable buffering times. But one day that all changed. In the late 90s, a breakthrough technology revolutionized streaming video, setting the foundation for the era of Netflix.

programme.title: The Lightbulb Moment
programme.desc: Dwight Eisenhower's interstate highway system was the biggest infrastructure project of its time, a bold plan to connect all corners of America and boost the economy. So how did it end up deepening the divide between rich and poor?

programme.title: Zenith: Advances in Space Exploration
programme.desc: This episode looks at the work of the probes orbiting Mars and the rovers exploring its surface.

programme.title: The Animal Communicator
programme.desc: Anna Breytenbach has dedicated her life to what she calls interspecies communication. She sends detailed messages to animals through pictures and thoughts. She then receives messages of remarkable clarity back from the animals.

programme.title: Wild Animal Rescue
programme.sub-title: Closing the Worst Zoo In The World
programme.desc: Animals at a struggling zoo in Gaza are in desperate need of aid. Left devastated by war, Khan Younis Zoo was dubbed the ‘worst zoo in the world.' In 2014 Khan Younis Zoo boasted 44 animals now only 15 remain. FOUR PAWS must rapidly swing into action

programme.title: The Great Penguin Rescue
programme.desc: Michaela rescues two cuddly little penguin chicks, but will she be able to care for them well enough to keep them alive through their critical first two weeks?

programme.title: The Great Penguin Rescue
programme.desc: As more starving penguin chicks are rescued, Michaela must work as hard as she can to keep the youngsters alive, but can she keep up with the pace as the birds keep on coming?

programme.title: The Great Penguin Rescue
programme.desc: Michael needs to face her fears now and learn the critical task of hand feeding young birds. If she doesn't, they will certainly die. But can she master the task in time?

programme.title: The Great Penguin Rescue
programme.desc: It's the end of her time at the rescue mission, now she must successfully release her favourite birds back into the sea. Have they learnt enough to survive in the wild? Michaela certainly hopes so.

programme.title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
programme.desc: Anchorage is home over 2000 moose! And these notorious animals are running riot. Crossing busy highways, giving birth in backyards, and even doing battle in suburbia! But life on the streets is fraught with danger in this expanding city.

programme.title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
programme.desc: Most of the year it's encased in ice but when summer hits, Anchorage bursts into life. Animals and plants living this far north have been forced to adapt, but the wildlife built to withstand Alaskan winters, often struggle in the summer heat!

programme.title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
programme.desc: Alaska is one of the last true wildernesses on earth. But in Anchorage you don't have to head into to the wild to discover the wildlife. With a city that has so much green space, it means everywhere is fair game for its wild neighbours.

programme.title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
programme.desc: With so many animals living in the city, sometimes they get themselves into trouble. But help is never far away. In Anchorage the wildlife come first, with lots of organisations and people that are working hard to protect them.

programme.desc: Host Amanda Herrera prepares a frittata, rosemary skewer chicken and grilled bread salad with fresh ingredients from a local farmers' market.

programme.title: Broccoli, Rainbow Chard and Herbs
programme.desc: Warm host Amanda Herrera picks up colourful produce and herbs at a farmers' market to make a delicious appetizer and tasty cold oil French fries.

programme.title: Honeycomb, Shallots and Iceberg Lettuce
programme.desc: Host Amanda Herrera transforms fresh market ingredients into two irresistible dishes, including a one pot Lamb Curry with Honeycomb.

programme.title: Patty Pan Summer Squash and Cabbage
programme.desc: Host Amanda Herrera grills up two dinner recipes using patty pan summer squash and cabbage that she picked up from the market.

programme.title: Cantonese Comfort Foods
programme.desc: What better way to feel good than delightful Cantonese comfort food? Talented home cook Mary Tang give us the lowdown on how to make crunchy, flavoursome Shrimp Toast. To spice it up, she boils a simmering hot pot of a traditional Cantonese soup - Congee. Normally eaten at breakfast, this delicious silky rice soup is loaded with succulent ground pork, earthy shiitake mushrooms, and aromatic ginger and scallions. Next, Mary swears that White Cut Chicken is the ultimate Cantonese comfort food. Slow-poached in juicy chicken stock paired with fresh broccoli and topped with fiery gingERS-scallion oil, this dish adds a little extra warmth to your life. For dessert, Mary whips up a batch of sweet, cloud-like Coconut Pudding that will surly melt-in-your-mouth.

programme.title: No Cutlery Required
programme.desc: Say goodbye to dirty cutlery in this One World Kitchen 3 episode featuring Vietnamese finger foods! Talented cook Lisa Nguyen starts off by making delicious Pork Skewer Rolls - perfect for any party! Featuring tender pork, crispy lettuce, fresh mint, crunchy cucumber, and Chinese chives wrapped in soft rice paper, this dish pairs well with a nuoc cham dipping sauce. Sticky Lemongrass Spare Ribs are next up to the plate steamed and marinated in a garlicy, caramel glaze (yum!). Next, Lisa fries up some tasty Shrimp and Sweet Potato Fritters using a foolproof technique, adding nuoc cham dipping sauce and lovely Bibb lettuce on the side. Finally, fingERS-licking Vietnamese Coffee Cupcakes will have you drooling (literally!). Sweetened condensed milk and rich sour cream paired with extra strong coffee will surly keep you coming back for more.

programme.title: Hot and Cold Recipes
programme.desc: Warm summer weather in Lebanon calls for less time in a piping hot kitchen! Fun host Jasmin Ibrahim prepares a refreshing salad with crunchy pita crisps called Fattoush and pairs it with a juicy chicken kebab. Then, she shows us a traditional Lebanese dish for a rainy day - Lentil Noodle Soup. With delicious brown lentils, warm and citrusy coriander, and tasty Lebanese pita noodles, this dish is sure to warm up your belly. Next, Jasmin cooks up a classic dish from the ancient Mediterranean coastal city of Tripoli. This traditional recipe, called Sayadiyeh, features a crispy fried cod and fluffy rice sprinkled in toasted almond slices. For dessert, Jasmin shows us one of her favourites, Sweet Cheese Rolls. Sweet, cheesy dough with a creamy milk curd filling and sticky syrup dip will have you making a second batch for later.

programme.title: City of Venice, Italy (Part 2)

programme.title: Village Baduy, Indonesia

programme.title: Sifnos, Cyclades island, Greece
programme.desc: An elegant island, symbolic of the Cyclades, discover the Greek island of Sifnos, full of contrasts, where the most arid mountains rub shoulders with the greenest valleys, and where monasteries dominate sandy beaches.

programme.title: Vannes Old City, France
programme.desc: Old City Walls of Vannes are fortifications built between the 3rd and 17th centuries to protect Vannes' city. It is located on the Gulf of Morbihan at the mouth of two rivers, the Marle and the Vincin, around 450 km southwest of Paris. These ancient city walls are some of the best-preserved in the country.

programme.title: Tallinn town, Estonia
programme.desc: Entering now in the Old Town of Tallinn, we understand how it became the best-preserved medieval city in Northern Europe, boasting Gothic spires, winding cobblestone streets, and enchanting architecture.Tallinn, the capital of Estonia, is a city out of a fairytale with centuries of history and traditions.

programme.title: Church of Nativity, Israel
programme.desc: The Church of The Nativity is situated in Betlehem to the south of Jerusalem.In 327 the church of Nativity was commissioned by Constantine the Great and his mother Helena over the site that was traditionally considered to be located over the cave that mar"&"ks the birthplace of Jesus…

programme.title: Temple of Sri Kailawasanathan Swami Devasthanam, Sri Lanka
programme.desc: Temple of Sri Kailawasanathan Swami Devasthanam Kovil is located in Colombo. The temple of Sri Kailawasanathan Swami DevasthanamKovil is one of the most beautiful and lovely attraction places of Sri Lanka. There are many beautiful and elegant paintings of God and Goddesss. Also there are an beautiful carvings of an astrology chart.

programme.title: Palazzo Ducale di Urbino, Italy
programme.desc: Hidden in the hills of Italy lies Palazzo Ducale di Urbino, the Ducal Palace of Urbino. It may seem strange to find such grand Renaissance architecture outside of the great cities. We all know about Rome, Venice, Florence and their great works. But the Ducal Palace of Urbino is often forgotten, despite rivaling all of them with its splendor and great history.

programme.title: Mir Castle Complex, Belarus
programme.desc: Located in the small town center, Mir Castle's complex is one of the earliest monuments to exist to this day and the most essential. This centuries-old Castle, reflected magnificently in an adjoining pond, resembles a building straight out of fairy tales. The common name 'mir' shared by the Castle and the town literally translates to 'peace' this justifies the fact that the Castle's sole purpose was built to bring harmony and peace to the region, followed by the signing of several peace treaties.

programme.title: Ling Sen Tong Temple, Malaysia
programme.desc: Follow the path that leads you to a picturesque garden with a beautiful pond at the centre. Located at the bottom of a limestone hill in Ipoh in Malaysia, Ling Sen Tong temple is a magnificent Taoist cave temple. Decorated with various statues of deities, animals and other Chinese folklore characters amidst the stalagmites and stalactites, this temple boasts of a majestic structure...

programme.title: HOTELS in Madrid
programme.desc: Would you prefer a prestigious palace, an eco-lodge in the middle of the jungle, a charming villa or a design temple in an urban setting? LUXURY DREAMS takes you on a voyage of discovery of rare addresses, always top luxury, often quite unusual. Today direction Hong Kong.

programme.title: Lisbon Historic Center, Portugal, Part 2
programme.desc: One of the most charismatic, vibrant, and picturesque cities in Europe: Lisbon, Portugal's capital. It is a city where a rich historical past, striking modernism, and progressive thinking are closely and organically combined.

programme.title: Penang area, Malaysia
programme.desc: Penang's Malaysian state's main constituent island is a region with a unique blend of Malay, Chinese, Indian and British cultures and traditions, with a vibrant history. Overflowing with charming historic buildings and ornate religious sites, the island r"&"esembles a magnificent city from the past entirely. Located at the Ayer Itam suburbs foot in George Town, also called Penang, and the leading port of Malaysia, Kek Lok Si temple is known as the jewel in Malaysia's crown of heritage temples. The temple com"&"plex expands from the very temple, which is considered one of the largest and finest temple complexes in Southeast Asia. The complex grows over approximately 30 acres donated by Yeoh Siew Beow, who became the temple's first abbot.

programme.title: Mdina town, Malta
programme.desc: Factual documentary about Mdina town, Malta.Mdina also known by its titles Città Vecchia or Città Notabile, is a fortified city in the Northern Region of Malta which served as the island's capital from antiquity to the medieval period. The city is still c"&"onfined within its walls, and has a population of just under 300, but it is contiguous with the town of Rabat, which takes its name from the Arabic word for suburb, and has a population of over 11,000 (as of March 2014). The city was founded as Maleth in "&"around the 8th century BC by Phoenician settlers, and was later renamed Melite by the Romans. Ancient Melite was larger than present-day Mdina, and it was reduced to its present size during the Byzantine or Arab occupation of Malta. During the latter peri"&"od, the city adopted its present name, which derives from the Arabic word medina. The city remained the capital of Malta throughout the Middle Ages, until the arrival of the Order of St. John in 1530, when Birgu became the administrative centre of the isl"&"and. Mdina experienced a period of decline over the following centuries, although it saw a revival in the early 18th century. At this point, it acquired several Baroque features, although it did not lose its medieval character.Mdina remained the centre of"&" the Maltese nobility and religious authorities, but it never regained its pre-1530 importance, giving rise to the popular nickname the ""Silent City"" by both locals and visitors. Mdina is on the tentative list of UNESCO World Heritage Sites, and it is n"&"ow one of the main tourist attractions in Malta. Today, Mdina is one of Malta's major tourist attractions, hosting about 750,000 tourists a year. No cars are allowed in Mdina, partly why it has earned the nickname 'the Silent City'. The city displays an u"&"nusual mix of Norman and Baroque architecture, including several palaces, most of which serve as private homes.An extensive restoration of the city walls was undertaken between 2008 and 2016.

programme.title: Skopje A Blend Of Heritage, North Macedonia
programme.desc: While easily accessible from all points abroad and boasting all the amenities of the Western world, North Macedonia is still one of Europe's least discovered tourist destinations: a natural paradise of mountains, lakes, and rivers, where life moves to a "&"different rhythm, amidst the sprawling grandeur of rich historical ruins and idyllic villages that have remained practically unchanged for centuries. Skopje is the capital of the Republic of Macedonia in the center of the Balkan Peninsula. In different pe"&"riods of its history, the city was under Roman, Byzantine, and Ottoman rule.

programme.title: Sveti Juraj, Montenegro
programme.desc: Perast is an old town on the Bay of Kotor in Montenegro. It is situated a few kilometres northwest of Kotor and is noted for its proximity to the islets of St. George and Our Lady of the Rocks.The city was founded by the Illyrians, named after one of the "&"local tribes—Pirusti.Perast lies beneath the hill of St. Ilija (873 m), on a cape that separates the Bay of Risano from that of Kotor, and overlooks the Verige strait, the narrowest part of the Bay of Kotor.

programme.title: Orhei Vechi Monastery Moldova
programme.desc: Old Orhei is a Moldovan historical and archaeological complex located in Trebujeni, which is approximately 60 kilometres (37 mi) north-east of Chişinău on the Răut River in the Republic of Moldova. The ancient city of Orheiul Vechi is a natural and histo"&"rical complex, located in a narrow bend of the Raut River. The natural landscape of limestone rock, eroded by the river, is combined with archaeological vestiges of the ancient Trypillian civilization. The Orthodox monastery is still inhabited by a handfu"&"l of Orthodox monks, who maintain the church at the top of the hill. The caves are still functional as chapels. The caves contain an array of historical artifacts.

programme.title: Ein Kerem, The Christian Revelation, Israel
programme.desc: Ein Kerem, the place of the birth of Saint John the Baptist, as well as the visitation of Mary, but also a chic and pastoral touristic place…

programme.title: Burano town, Italy
programme.desc: Burano is an island in the Venetian Lagoon, northern Italy, near Torcello at the northern end of the lagoon, known for its lace work and brightly coloured homes. The primary economy is tourism. Burano is 7 kilometres (4 miles) from Venice, a 45-minute tri"&"p from St. Mark's Square by vaporetto, a Venetian water bus.The island is linked to Mazzorbo by a bridge. The current population of Burano is about 2,400.

programme.title: Bosa Old town, Sardinia, Italy
programme.desc: The beautiful Bosa Old Town finds itself in Alghero To Oristano, an area strongly influenced by Spanish culture. The city is generally considered to have been founded in 1102. However, long before the Christian era, the Phoenicians set up a trading post o"&"n this exposed bay in western Sardinia.

programme.title: Corinaldo old town, Italy
programme.desc: Let yourself be captivated by Corinaldo, an old city in the Italian region of Marche. Let's discover its powerful walls, its archaeological past, its rural landscape and the mythical Corinaldo's Fools. The lush and central Italian region of Marche is the "&"host of what has been dubbed, literally, Italy's prettiest village. Nestled within the Province of Ancona, Corinaldo's picturesque alleyways tell a story hundreds of years old.
